textByHash.0490d377b35faed9fe52a4afa2bd5e86e5306630: His paltry reign is sadly no more.
textByHash.078df5ff612dfce22e9289582fa7d3946ea7853c: That was his name! CHICKEN KING! By the light, he fought valiantly.
textByHash.37423e379798b94785139d6e57d5baa738ca9c4b: He screamed 'Off with their heads' while flailing around with a Halberd...
textByHash.38ddb1ecc4dd594f76a81d0522643ba72b2d7b84: She don't speak much, not since the incident.
textByHash.50618b3549fab804e0f4efe7b4a3979562481b80: During our journey we came upon an abandoned house, it was eerily... silent.
textByHash.6ecaec3857b71f21784bb1e687189b669b662f27: Between the mountains we witnessed the Chicken King invade a mountain fort full of Mount Tinmen.
textByHash.7608547f25a0fffe2b4c1f797f3a6e5c579bd47e: I'd prefer not to go into details, but I will say this...
textByHash.872d5272432d06ff96219c97f51246715ab68458: The entire family was dead but there was not a single mark on them... Makes you think, what could do such a thing?
textByHash.c19ac31317c289dd4c89213bdee969846079c3ef: Such a waste... Such a pretty garden.
textByHash.c966776c857d6d11511f26bc32e7740b08497240: As we entered the house we were hit by a horrendous smell - it was quite surprising.
textByHash.ee7ca67898fbdb7af82c184d257f03d32e800c6b: Not right now... ask me later.
